matrix.source_summary: explicitly listed in features for Privy, Magic, Turnkey, Crossmint, Dynamic, Web3Auth, Thirdweb (as in-app wallets), Portal; Alchemy and Particle offer wallet APIs/universal accounts rather than classic embedded wallets
Magic features list (express + core API), Turnkey wallet-as-a-service, Dynamic and Thirdweb features lists; Privy has server-side sessions but not explicitly branded as server wallets
Turnkey differentiators (first-class agentic wallets), Crossmint features (agent wallets and cards), Dynamic features (agent payments), Thirdweb differentiators (Infrastructure for AI Agents)
Dynamic (ERC-4337 & EIP-7702 smart wallets), Web3Auth features list, Thirdweb features, Portal features, Alchemy bundler API, Particle (ERC-4337) features lists
Magic, Turnkey, Dynamic, Web3Auth, Portal all explicitly list non-custodial key management in features or differentiators; Privy inferred from TEE + key sharding architecture
Web3Auth explicitly lists MPC and MPC TSS; Portal lists TSS MPC and enclave MPC API; Dynamic lists TSS-MPC in differentiators; Privy uses TEEs not MPC
Privy differentiators (hardware-isolated TEEs), Magic features (TEE + AWS Nitro), Portal integrations (AWS Nitro Enclaves); Turnkey has Verifiable Cloud in beta
Privy features list (key sharding), Magic features list (key sharding + customizable sharding in differentiators); Web3Auth uses MPC-based sharding implicitly
Magic features (transaction signing + unlimited signatures), Turnkey differentiators (millisecond signing), Portal features (raw signature), Dynamic (sub-second signing)
Crossmint features list, Portal features list, Alchemy features list all explicitly include transaction simulation
Alchemy features list (transaction batching explicit), Particle features list (batched transactions explicit); others offer it implicitly via ERC-4337 smart wallets
Privy, Crossmint, Thirdweb, Alchemy, Particle all explicitly list gas sponsorship in features lists
Turnkey differentiators (pre-generated wallets), Crossmint features (wallet pre-generation), Dynamic features (pre-generated wallets), Web3Auth features (wallet pregeneration)
Magic features (key export), Turnkey features (wallet import and export), Portal features (eject feature for export); Dynamic has migration tools
All providers explicitly list multi-chain support in features; Thirdweb claims 2500+ chains, Portal claims 100+, Alchemy claims 80+ networks
explicitly listed in all 10 competitor features lists; privy/magic/thirdweb/portal each name it directly
explicitly named in privy, magic, crossmint, web3auth, thirdweb, portal, alchemy features; Turnkey and Dynamic silent
explicitly in privy, magic, dynamic (bitcoin embedded wallets), web3auth features; majority of competitors silent
explicitly in privy, turnkey, crossmint, dynamic features; magic via Newton Protocol differentiator; portal via smart contract firewall only
explicitly in privy (multi-approver quorums, multisig support) and turnkey features; thirdweb via Safe integration only
explicitly in crossmint features and turnkey (delegated access); privy via smart wallet signers; alchemy/dynamic via EIP-7702 only
only Turnkey explicitly names sub-organizations; it is a core Turnkey differentiator with no equivalent naming elsewhere
explicitly in privy (server-side sessions), magic, turnkey, dynamic, web3auth features lists
Particle Network positions chain abstraction as first-class primitive with universal accounts; others offer multi-chain without true abstraction layer
only Turnkey explicitly lists HD wallet support; most competitors use MPC/TEE architectures that replace HD derivation
explicitly in turnkey and thirdweb features; partial via smart accounts/AA in dynamic, web3auth, portal, alchemy
explicitly listed in privy, crossmint, dynamic, thirdweb, alchemy features lists
explicitly in crossmint (onramps/offramps), dynamic (on & off ramps), portal (on/off ramp integration); magic and web3auth on-ramp only
explicitly in privy (automated bridging), thirdweb (bridge and swap), portal (swaps) features; particle abstraction layer replaces bridging
explicitly in dynamic (earn/yield, 1-click yield integration) and portal (yield integrations) features; all others silent
features list for Portal ('card issuing') and Crossmint ('agent wallets and cards'); Dynamic has stablecoin cards as an integration only
Crossmint features list explicitly names 'apple pay and google pay'; no other source confirms
Crossmint features list explicitly names 'bank transfers'; others have fiat on/off-ramps which may include ACH/wire but are not confirmed as bank transfers specifically
Privy features ('wallet connectors'), Magic features ('external wallet login'), Dynamic differentiators ('600+ external wallets'), Web3Auth features ('third-party wallet connectors') all confirm
Crossmint ('token checkout', 'batch minting') and Web3Auth ('nft checkout', 'nft minting') explicitly confirm; Thirdweb and Alchemy have token/NFT APIs but not checkout flows
Thirdweb features ('mcp server'), Alchemy features ('mcp server'), Particle features ('mcp server'), Dynamic integrations ('Cursor (MCP), Claude Code (MCP)') all explicitly confirm
Turnkey features ('stablecoin infrastructure support'), Crossmint features ('stablecoin orchestration'), Dynamic features ('stablecoin accounts') all explicitly confirm
Portal features ('walletconnect integration') and Dynamic integrations ('WalletConnect') explicitly confirm; no other source names it directly
Only Thirdweb features explicitly names 'storage (ipfs)'; no other source confirms
Only Thirdweb features explicitly names 'ai model (blockchain-specialized)'; Crossmint mentions 'ai assistant integration' but that is tooling, not a proprietary model

Magic (Newton Protocol), Turnkey (agentic wallets), Crossmint (agentic checkout/agent wallets), Dynamic (agentic finance infra), Thirdweb (infra for AI agents), Alchemy (ai agents support) all explicitly confirm

Crossmint features explicitly names 'agentic checkout'; Thirdweb's x402 protocol covers agentic payments; Dynamic has 'agent payments'; Zergwallet spec has policy-gated agent transfers but no checkout flow

Privy features ('rbac with micro-segmentation') and Alchemy features ('rbac') explicitly confirm; others have policy engines, scoped API access, or team seats that imply role separation without naming RBAC
Privy features, Magic features, and Crossmint differentiators all explicitly name SOC 2 Type II; Portal mentions 'certification and audits' without specifying the standard
Magic features explicitly names ISO 27001:2022, GDPR, and CCPA; Crossmint differentiators name GDPR and MiCA authorization; Privy lists 'compliance tools' without specifics
crossmint features list (advanced aml screening, transaction monitoring) + Elliptic integration; all others absent
crossmint features list (travel rule compliance) + NotaBene integration; no other source mentions it
crossmint features list (kyc data imports via api); no other source explicitly mentions KYC
privy differentiators (HackerOne bug bounty + 5 named audits), dynamic features list, portal features list (certification and audits), particle features list
magic.link and thirdweb features lists (team seats explicit); dynamic has user management, alchemy has RBAC
turnkey (compliance audit trail), dynamic (audit and event logs), thirdweb (audit logs), alchemy (account activity logs); us_spec explicitly describes audit log with pagination and CSV export

dynamic features list (allowlists, rate limiting explicit), alchemy features list (ip/domain allowlisting); crossmint has anti-spam whitelisting only; us_spec has recipient whitelist in policy evaluator
turnkey features list (enterprise disaster recovery explicit); portal differentiators describe eject feature as user-controlled DR redundancy
crossmint differentiators (four 9s SLAs), eb3auth features list, thirdweb (infrastructure sla), portal features list, alchemy differentiators (99.99% uptime SLA)
crossmint features list (chargeback protection explicit); dynamic features list has fraud protection but no chargeback
portal features list (smart contract firewall explicit); crossmint has transaction simulation via Blockaid but not a named firewall product

privy differentiators (whitelabel wallets), magic features list, dynamic features list, eb3auth (whitelabel login ui), portal (full whitelabel); crossmint limited to onramps; thirdweb limited to branding removal
privy features list (modular ui components), magic (wallet ui widgets), dynamic (ui components/sdk), eb3auth (customizable modal, wallet ui, plug n play sdk)
privy (passkey support), magic (passkey login), turnkey (passkey authentication), eb3auth (passkeys), portal (passkey authentication) — all explicit in features lists
privy, magic, turnkey, dynamic, eb3auth, particle all list social login explicitly; thirdweb not explicit in provided source; alchemy google oauth is dashboard-level only
features lists for Magic, Web3Auth, Particle Network; Privy progressive authentication implies email
features lists for Magic (sms login), Web3Auth (sms otp), Thirdweb (sms delivery), Privy (sms marketing implies sms infra)

features lists for Magic (multi-factor authentication, advanced mfa), Dynamic (multi-factor authentication), Web3Auth (multi-factor authentication, advanced mfa methods), Privy (hardware-backed login and mfa)
features lists for Magic (bring-your-own auth provider), Crossmint (bring your own auth), Dynamic (bring your own auth / jwt), Web3Auth (custom authentication)
features lists for Magic (sso), Dynamic (sso / okta support), Alchemy (sso)
features lists for Magic (token gating) and Dynamic (token gating)
features list for Web3Auth (telegram mini app login) and Portal (telegram wallet embedding)
features lists for Magic (custom email provider smtp, custom email templates), Crossmint (custom email receipts), Web3Auth (custom domains), Thirdweb (custom branding for emails)
explicitly listed in Web3Auth features; stated as differentiator for Dynamic, Portal, Particle; implied by TEE/MPC architecture across Privy, Magic, Turnkey, Crossmint, Thirdweb
only Crossmint explicitly lists verifiable credentials in features; absent from all other sources
Magic explicitly lists onchain automation; server wallets and programmable APIs across all vendors imply automation; Zergwallet spec confirms policy-gated agent autonomy with real Sepolia transfers
Crossmint explicitly lists transaction queueing and reliability engine; Zergwallet spec confirms pending-actions queue with admin approve/reject
Magic explicitly lists massively parallel signing; Turnkey differentiator states 'sign millions of transactions at millisecond speed'; Portal differentiator mentions fast signing speeds
Crossmint explicitly lists batch minting and airdrops to emails and social accounts; Thirdweb has token creation tooling but batch minting not explicitly listed

policy engine present across Privy, Magic, Turnkey, Crossmint, Dynamic; account abstraction and programmable account logic explicit in Particle Network; Zergwallet spec confirms policy evaluator with per-token/asset/tx/daily limits
features list for Turnkey (explicit), stablecoin orchestration for Crossmint, onchain automation for Magic, x402 payments for Thirdweb
features list for Dynamic (explicit), multi-signature approvals and root quorum for Turnkey, pending-actions queue in Zergwallet spec
features list for Web3Auth (community forum, office hours, dedicated support), Portal (Slack developer support), Particle (Slack community)
features list for Portal (explicit), no other sources confirm
features list for Privy (explicit), analytics dashboards for Crossmint, user management & analytics for Dynamic, wallet API debugging dashboard for Alchemy
features list for Alchemy (custom error and usage alerts, usage reporting explicit)
features list for Alchemy (explicit), unique to Alchemy among all surveyed sources
features list for Magic, Web3Auth, Portal (explicit iOS SDK); Crossmint lists generic mobile SDKs; Dynamic lists react native / mobile SDK
features list for Magic, Web3Auth, Portal (explicit Android SDK plus PortalWebView for Android)
features list for Magic, Dynamic, Web3Auth, Thirdweb, Portal (all explicit React Native SDK)
features lists for Magic, Dynamic, Web3Auth, Thirdweb, Portal confirm SDKs spanning web, mobile, and game engines (Flutter, Unity, Unreal)
features list for Portal (explicit multi-platform syncing); Dynamic global wallets and Particle universal accounts are cross-chain not cross-device
features list for Crossmint (cli tool), Thirdweb (cli), Alchemy (alchemy cli) all explicit
features list for Thirdweb (faucets and chainlist explicit), Alchemy (rollup testnet and multi-chain sandbox, own infrastructure only)
features list for Dynamic (explicit), no other sources confirm
Alchemy features list ('rollups, rollup testnet, rollup mainnet'); no other source mentions it
Crossmint features list ('currency and language localization'); only source to name it explicitly
Particle Network features list ('open-source sdks'); Privy notes 'open source cryptography' only — partial
Alchemy (transfers/token/nft/portfolio APIs as core product), Thirdweb ('insight — blockchain data api'), Crossmint ('indexing and data apis'); Privy lists 'webhooks and indexing' as partial

drift.note: spec aspiration — the core front-door differentiator (agent moves money inside per-asset/per-token/per-day caps with approval queue) is not surfaced on the live site at all.

drift.feature: multi-rail aggregation (crypto + brokerage + neobank + business banking)
drift.note: spec aspiration — live site shows no mention of connecting external accounts across rails (Mercury, Coinbase, BTC xpub, EVM watch).
spec aspiration — always-on CFO/CPA/CFA AI chat panel reading per-turn account snapshot is absent from the live site.
spec aspiration — audit log capturing every agent/user action with reasoning, pagination, and CSV export is not mentioned on the live site.
spec aspiration — BIP84 xpub 40-derived-address aggregation is a shipped spec feature with no live-site surface or mention.

drift.feature: evm multi-chain support (arbitrum / base / op mainnet / polygon / sepolia)
drift.note: spec aspiration — six supported EVM chains are specified but not communicated anywhere on the live site.
spec aspiration — webhook infrastructure for transfer events is built but not surfaced or marketed on the live site.
spec aspiration — Mercury REST read integration exists in spec but is invisible on the live site; outbound is explicitly deferred.
spec aspiration — Coinbase read-only scaffold exists but is explicitly deferred and unfinished; live site shows nothing.

drift.feature: double-entry ledger / custodial source of truth
drift.note: marketing gap — the internal double-entry ledger with atomic org transfers is a real shipped primitive but is not communicated as a trust/safety differentiator on the live site.
spec aspiration — Mercury+Brex+QuickBooks replacement positioning for founders and small teams is in the spec but absent from live site copy.
